question:
Will Japan win Group F in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

Resolution criteria: Resolves to the outcome of the question found at https://polymarket.com/market/will-japan-win-group-f-in-the-2026-fifa-world-cup.

Background: This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group F in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Group F
evidences.content: ## Discipline [edit] The team conduct ("fair play") score would have been used as a tiebreaker if the head-to-head and overall records of teams were tied. It would also be used as a tiebreaker for the third-place ranking between groups if the overall records of teams were tied. The score was calculated based on yellow and red cards received by players and team officials in all group matches as follows: yellow card: −1 point; indirect red card (second yellow card): −3 points; direct red card: −4 points; yellow card and direct red card: −5 points; Only one of the above deductions could be applied to a player or team official in a single match. [...] Notes 1. ↑ The rankings of November 2025 were used for seeding for the final draw. 2. 1 2 As the identity of the UEFA second round Path B winner was not known at the time of the final draw, positions in the FIFA Rankings were not taken into account, and the placeholder in the draw was automatically seeded into pot 4. ## Standings [edit] | Pos | Team v t e | Pld | W | D | L | GF | GA | GD | Pts | Qualification | --- --- --- --- --- | 1 | Netherlands | 3 | 2 | 1 | 0 | 10 | 4 | +6 | 7 | Advance to knockout stage | | 2 | Japan | 3 | 1 | 2...

evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Format, groups, full match schedule, more
evidences.content: The knockout stage consists of a series of winner-take-all matches, all the way through to the final. If a match is tied after full time, 30 minutes of added extra time are played as two 15-minute halves, with a penalty shootout as the ultimate decider. ## What are the groups for the World Cup? Click the arrows at the top to scroll through all of the World Cup groups. visualization ## What are the tiebreakers for the group stage? If two or more teams finish equal on group stage points, the following tiebreakers are applied in order. 1. Most points obtained in the group matches played between the teams concerned 2. Superior goal difference in the group matches played between the teams concerned 3. Most goals scored in the group matches played between the teams concerned [...] If any teams remain tied, the same tiebreakers are applied again, exclusively to the matches between those teams. From there, ties are broken by the following criteria. 4. Superior goal difference in all group matches 5. Most goals scored in all group matches 6. Highest team conduct ("fair play") score in all group matches 7. FIFA world ranking - World Cup 2026 qualifying: Who has qualified, and how the rest...

evidences.title: 2026 World Cup tiebreak rules: What happens if teams finish ...
evidences.content: ## Does head-to-head or goal difference come first? Head-to-head results come before goal difference at the 2026 World Cup. FIFA’s regulations prioritize the direct results between tied teams before considering their overall performance in the group. Advertisement Under the current system, if Team A and Team B finish level on points and Team A defeated Team B during the group stage, Team A would rank higher regardless of overall goal difference, provided the head-to-head criteria resolve the tie. The rule has already had a noticeable impact during the 2026 tournament. Several teams have clinched qualification or group victories before the final matchday because their head-to-head victories guaranteed an advantage over direct rivals. [...] The first set of criteria focuses exclusively on the matches played between the tied teams. FIFA examines the number of points earned in those head-to-head meetings, followed by head-to-head goal difference and head-to-head goals scored. Advertisement This is a significant change from previous World Cups, where overall goal difference was the primary tiebreaker. If the teams remain level after those head-to-head criteria are applied, FIFA moves...

evidences.title: 2026 World Cup preview: Groups A-F - by Clemente Lisi
evidences.content: Brazil, a five-time World Cup champion, enters the tournament carrying the weight of expectation. France, runners-up four years ago, and Spain, the defending European champions, continue to produce elite talent at an astonishing rate. Defending champion Argentina, even with an older Lionel Messi, still possesses enough quality to defend its title. England, meanwhile, believe this could finally be its moment. What makes this World Cup fascinating is the middle tier. Morocco no longer looks like a Cinderella story after its semifinal run in 2022. Japan has become a serious tactical force. Norway dominated the qualifiers and will look to make a deep run. Belgium and the Netherlands are once again in the mix, although how seriously they can content for the trophy remains to be seen. [...] Group F (Netherlands, Japan, Sweden and Tunisia) Both the Dutch and Japan headline a compelling Group F. The Netherlands again appears to enter as a dark horse (despite the absence to the injured Xavi Simons). Virgil van Dijk still anchors the defense, bringing the team an amount of certainty and confidence. The inclusion of Memphis Depay, still recovering from an injured wrist, appears to be a ris...

evidences.title: What are the World Cup's group-stage tie-breakers?
evidences.content: After head-to-head record, goal difference in head-to-head matches is the second tie-breaker used. If that is level, it goes down to which of the teams involved scored the most goals in head-to-head matches. If that is also level, the next tie-breaker is goal difference from the entire group stage. It then moves to goals scored in all group-stage matches. Should that also be tied, it goes to which team has the better "team conduct score" - effectively meaning who has had the cleanest disciplinary record. In the event that all of those tie-breakers still cannot be solved, the final tie-breaker is by ranking teams by the latest Fifa world rankings, which are updated after every international match. ## Every group stage tie-breaker for the 2026 World Cup [...] # What are the World Cup's group-stage tie-breakers? AMA banner If two or more teams are tied on points in the same group in the 2026 World Cup, head-to-head record is used as the first tie-breaker. This is a change from previous World Cups, when goal difference was the initial tie-breaker. Head-to-head record was not used as any tie-breaker until 1994 but has now been made the first deciding factor. It means that should mult...

evidences.title: Japan 2026 World Cup Preview: Samurai Blue Target a Quarterfinal Berth
evidences.content: Takefusa Kubo is key to Japan’s hopes. | Juan Luis Medina # Japan 2026 World Cup Preview: Samurai Blue Target a Quarterfinal Berth Record Asian champion Japan is aiming to upset the established order once again after a memorable 2022 campaign. Ewan Ross-Murray| Google DiscoverAdd us as a preferred source Takefusa Kubo is key to Japan’s hopes. | Juan Luis Medina Japan has a proud and storied history at the World Cup since first appearing in 1998, qualifying for every subsequent tournament and securing some memorable results. [...] Two of those came in 2022 when the Samurai Blue conquered both Germany and Spain during the group stage, but falling to Croatia in the last 16 penalty shootout meant another tournament without reaching the quarterfinals. Japan will hope to make the unprecedented journey to the last eight for its first World Cup in North America. Hajime Moriyasu’s roster lacks an individual superstar, but boasts an abundance of talent up and down the field—especially in attack. Japan’s team cohesion and unwavering commitment to the cause can help them spring more surprises this summer. ## The Road to the World Cup Qualification record: 13W–1L-2D Goals for / against:54 /...

evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup tiebreakers: How can teams advance to the knockout rounds? | MLSSoccer.com
evidences.content: All-Star Tickets Slim Alert Major League Soccer # 2026 FIFA World Cup tiebreakers: How can teams advance to the knockout rounds? 2026WC-ball ## share The 2026 FIFA World Cup has expanded to 48 nations, creating a brand new qualification structure for the knockout phase. In the updated format, the top two teams from each group and the top eight third-place finishers advance to the Round of 32. So, how is third-place advancement determined? And what happens if teams are tied on points at the end of the group stage? Let's take a look at all of the group stage tiebreakers: ### How do points work? In the group stage, teams are awarded three points for a win, one point for a draw and zero points for a loss. [...] In turn, two or more teams may be tied on points at the end of the three-game group stage, requiring tiebreakers to determine final positioning. ### What are tiebreakers? If two or more teams finish equal on group-stage points, the order of the tiebreakers below determines who finishes on top: ### How do third-place teams advance? In addition to the top two finishers in each group, the top eight third-place finishers across all groups will advance to the knockout stage to com...

evidences.title: 2026 World Cup: Picking winners, runners-up for all 12 ...
evidences.content: Group F winner: Netherlands Group F runner-up: Japan ## Group G: Belgium, Egypt, Iran, New Zealand Four years ago, Belgium surprisingly went out in the group stage, but it’s hard to see the Red Devils repeating that feat. I’ll stick with the favored Belgians to win this group, and then it’s a coin flip between Egypt and Iran (sorry, New Zealand). Egypt has the star power of Mo Salah and Omar Marmoush on the wings. Iran excelled in AFC qualifying with a veteran squad that has a lower ceiling but a higher floor, and I’ll take Mehdi Taremi and Co. to go through in second place. Group G winner: Belgium Group G runner-up: Iran ## Group H: Spain, Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay [...] Group E winner: Ecuador Group E runner-up: Germany ## Group F: Netherlands, Japan, Sweden, Tunisia Deciding which teams to pick to advance is easy for me here, but settling on an order between Netherlands and Japan is difficult. Both are clearly more complete teams than Sweden and Tunisia. The Dutch took eventual champion Argentina to penalties four years ago, and they’re just as good or better now. Japan has a pleasing style and a strong Europe-strewn squad despite losing two starters to injury. Japan...

evidences.title: Odds to Win World Cup - 2026 FIFA World Cup Favorites
evidences.content: ## World Cup Favorites and Contenders France France is among the top favorites to win the 2026 World Cup. Les Bleus reached the Final at Qatar 2022 and continue to build around Kylian Mbappé. France's squad depth across every position is part of why oddsmakers consistently price them near the top of the board. Spain Spain entered the tournament as one of the shortest-priced contenders to win the World Cup. La Roja's possession-heavy system and elite La Liga talent make them one of the most balanced sides in the field, with a young attacking core including Lamine Yamal supporting an experienced midfield. [...] England England's odds to win the World Cup place them among the elite contenders. The Three Lions have reached at least the quarter-finals in each of the last four major tournaments, with Harry Kane leading the line as the team's all-time top goalscorer. Argentina Defending champions Argentina enter the tournament with strong odds to win back-to-back World Cups. Lionel Messi is expected to play in what will likely be his final World Cup, alongside Lautaro Martínez. Argentina's pedigree and recent tournament form keep them among the favorites. ## How World Cup Futures Odds...

evidences.title: Japan World Cup 2026 team guide | Japan | The Guardian
evidences.content: 25 June v Sweden, Dallas (6pm local, 26 June 12am BST, 26 June 9am AEST) Kaishu Sano is set to anchor the midfield and the fact that a player such as Takehiro Tomiyasu has to settle for a place on the bench at times is evidence that Japan’s squad depth has never been stronger. Injuries to Takumi Minamino, Wataru Endo and Kaoru Mitoma are very unfortunate but, again, this team are not so fragile as to collapse because of the absence of one or two missing star players. Crystal Palace’s Daichi Kamada will play an important role. Still, Group F will be tough with the two European sides, the Netherlands and Sweden, having strong squads while Tunisia may in fact turn out to be the most difficult stylistic matchup of the three. [...] The expected system is a 3-4-2-1, though Japan also experimented with a 3-1-4-2 against England, suggesting tactical flexibility depending on the oppostion. Pressing aggressively from the top is important, with players such as Takefusa Kubo, Ritsu Doan, Keito Nakamura and Junya Ito all excellent at putting pressure on opponents. Leading the line is Feyenoord’s Ayase Ueda, who won the Eredivisie Golden Boot in 2025-26, scoring an impressive 25 goals in 31 a...

evidences.title: Japan at the 2026 FIFA World Cup: JSoccer on Samurai Blue's Biggest Test Yet | Inside the Mind Of
evidences.content: Jamie McCreedy is joined by JSoccer for an in-depth preview of Japan's 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign. They discuss how the Samurai Blue became the first nation to qualify for the tournament, analyse Hajime Moriyasu's squad, and examine Japan's hopes of finally breaking their long-standing Round of 16 curse. The conversation covers key players including Takefusa Kubo, Wataru Endo, Takehiro Tomiyasu, Daichi Kamada and Ao Tanaka, the impact of injuries to Kaoru Mitoma and Takumi Minamino, Japan's challenging Group F fixtures against the Netherlands, Tunisia and Sweden, recent victories over top European opposition, and whether this talented squad has what it takes to make history at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
